piece.title: Shake the Disease (FormaComposition)
piece.notes: Structure: Intro → Verse → Chorus → Verse → Chorus → Bridge → Chorus → Outro. Verse: Dm-C-F-Bb (aeolian, warm). Chorus: Dm-Fm-Db-Bb (Fm is borrowed bIIIm — the DM signature dark move). Bridge: Bb-C-Bb-Dm. Primary melody carries the 'plea' motif throughout (rhythm:motif). Two counterpoint voices ride above/below: counterpoint 1 (arpeggio character, above) and counterpoint 2 (counterline character, below), each now rhythmically independent of the melody and of each other (free species own-rhythm generation). v3: harmony switches to rhythm:motif (the theme's 'plea' rhythm — 1.0/0.5/0.5/1.0/1.0, syncopated) in the choruses and bridge for real rhythmic chord-strike propulsion (Ob-La-Di Ob-La-Da / Shake the Disease style), while intro/verses/outro keep sustain or loose free density to leave room to breathe.

sections.name: intro
sections.notes: Bass pulse only. Primary melody (plea motif) enters as a fragment. Counterpoint voices silent here — sparse texture only. Harmony stays sustained, no strumming yet.

sections.name: verse_1
sections.notes: Dm-C-F-Bb feel. Primary melody carries plea motif, lyrical. Counterpoint 1 (arpeggio character) interweaves above; counterpoint 2 (counterline character) below — both now rhythmically independent. Harmony stays loose/sparse, leaving room under the vocal-style melody.

sections.name: chorus_1
sections.notes: The darkness hits. bVI is the Db major borrowed chord (one semitone below tonic). Primary melody develops the plea motif. Counterpoint 1 (arpeggio) above, counterpoint 2 (counterline) below, both rhythmically independent. Harmony now strikes on the plea motif's own syncopated rhythm (1.0/0.5/0.5/1.0/1.0) instead of sustaining — real rhythmic propulsion under the borrowed-chord darkness, Ob-La-Di-style chord hits.

sections.name: verse_2
sections.notes: Same feel as verse 1, motif transform applied to the primary melody. Counterpoint 1 slightly pulled back for variation. Harmony drops back to loose/sparse, same breathing space as verse 1.

sections.name: chorus_2
sections.notes: Same rhythmic strumming as chorus 1 — plea motif drives the chord hits.

sections.name: bridge
sections.notes: Bb-C-Bb-Dm. Tension and release. Primary melody sparse here; counterpoint 1 and 2 move in contrary motion, building toward the final chorus. Harmony switches to motif rhythm here too — the syncopated strike pattern builds forward momentum into the final chorus rather than coasting on loose density.

sections.name: chorus_final
sections.notes: Extended final chorus — 16 bars. Primary melody and both counterpoint voices at full intensity, peak interweave. Harmony at full rhythmic strumming — plea motif driving every chord hit, peak propulsion.

sections.name: outro
sections.notes: Voices dissolve in reverse order. Bass pedal on D. Primary melody (plea motif) fragments only; counterpoint 2 drops out first, then counterpoint 1. Harmony returns to sustain, mirroring the intro — strumming fades back into stillness.
